heading: Local agriculture market program
label: 1627c Local agriculture market program

children.heading: Beginning farmer or rancher
children.label: 1 Beginning farmer or rancher
children.text: The term “beginning farmer or rancher” has the meaning given the term in .section 1991(a) of this title

children.heading: Direct producer-to-consumer marketing
children.label: 2 Direct producer-to-consumer marketing
children.text: The term “direct producer-to-consumer marketing” has the meaning given the term “direct marketing from farmers to consumers” in .section 3002 of this title

children.heading: Family farm
children.label: 3 Family farm
children.text: The term “family farm” has the meaning given the term in .section 1632a(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: multiple organizations involved in the production, processing, and consumption of food; and

children.label: ii
children.text: local, Tribal, or State governments; and

children.label: B
children.text: addresses food and farm-related issues and needs within city, county, State, Tribal region, multicounty region, or other region designated by the food council or food system network.

children.label: i
children.text: 1 or more producers; or

children.label: ii
children.text: 1 or more entities, 100 percent of the ownership and control of which is held by 1 or more producers.

children.label: i
children.text: a partnership;

children.label: ii
children.text: a limited liability corporation;

children.label: iii
children.text: a limited liability partnership; and

children.label: iv
children.text: a corporation.

children.label: A
children.text: targets and strengthens the profitability and competitiveness of small and medium-sized farms and ranches that are structured as a family farm; and

children.label: B
children.text: obtains agreement from an eligible agricultural producer group, farmer or rancher cooperative, or majority-controlled producer-based business venture that is engaged in the value chain on a marketing strategy.

children.label: A
children.text: 1 or more eligible partners (as defined in subsection (e)(1)); and

children.label: B
children.text: 1 or more eligible entities (as defined in subsection (e)(1)).

children.heading: Program
children.label: 8 Program
children.text: The term “Program” means the Local Agriculture Market Program established under subsection (b).

children.heading: Regional food chain coordination
children.label: 9 Regional food chain coordination
children.text: The term “regional food chain coordination” means coordination and collaboration along the supply chain to increase connections between producers and markets.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 10 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Agriculture.

children.heading: Socially disadvantaged farmer or rancher
children.label: 11 Socially disadvantaged farmer or rancher
children.text: The term “socially disadvantaged farmer or rancher” has the meaning given the term in .section 2003(e) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: has undergone a change in physical state;

children.label: ii
children.text: was produced in a manner that enhances the value of the agricultural commodity or product, as demonstrated through a business plan that shows the enhanced value, as determined by the Secretary;

children.label: iii
children.text: is physically segregated in a manner that results in the enhancement of the value of the agricultural commodity or product;

children.label: iv
children.text: is a source of farm- or ranch-based renewable energy, including E–85 fuel; or

children.label: v
children.text: is aggregated and marketed as a locally produced agricultural food product; and

children.label: i
children.text: the customer base for the agricultural commodity or product is expanded; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a greater portion of the revenue derived from the marketing, processing, or physical segregation of the agricultural commodity or product is available to the producer of the commodity or product.

children.heading: Veteran farmer or rancher
children.label: 13 Veteran farmer or rancher
children.text: The term “veteran farmer or rancher” has the meaning given the term in .section 2279(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: direct producer-to-consumer marketing;

children.label: B
children.text: local and regional food markets and enterprises; and

children.label: C
children.text: value-added agricultural products;
connects and cultivates regional food economies through public-private partnerships;
supports the development of business plans, feasibility studies, and strategies for value-added agricultural production and local and regional food system infrastructure;
strengthens capacity and regional food system development through community collaboration and expansion of mid-tier value chains;
improves income and economic opportunities for producers and food businesses through job creation; and
simplifies the application processes and the reporting processes for the Program.
streamline the Program to better support the activities carried out by the recipient of a grant under the Program;
connect producers with local food markets and value-added agricultural product opportunities;
partner with cooperative extension services, as appropriate, to provide Program technical assistance and outreach to Program stakeholders; and
ensure that the Rural Business-Cooperative Service and Agricultural Marketing Service provide Program technical assistance and outreach to Program stakeholders.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Under the Program, the Secretary may, using funds made available under subsection (i), provide grants for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2023, in accordance with the purposes of the Program described in subsection (b), for the conduct of activities described in paragraph (2).

children.label: i
children.text: domestic direct producer-to-consumer marketing;

children.label: ii
children.text: farmers’ markets;

children.label: iii
children.text: roadside stands;

children.label: iv
children.text: agritourism activities,

children.label: v
children.text: community-supported agriculture programs; or

children.label: vi
children.text: online sales;

children.label: B
children.text: to support local and regional food business enterprises that engage as intermediaries in indirect producer-to-consumer marketing;

children.label: i
children.text: local and regional food products that are marketed locally or regionally; and

children.label: ii
children.text: value-added agricultural products;

children.label: D
children.text: to encourage the development of value-added agricultural products;

children.label: E
children.text: to assist with business development plans and feasibility studies;

children.label: F
children.text: to develop marketing strategies for producers of local food products and value-added agricultural products in new and existing markets;

children.label: G
children.text: to facilitate regional food chain coordination and mid-tier value chain development;

children.label: H
children.text: to promote new business opportunities and marketing strategies to reduce on-farm food waste;

children.label: I
children.text: to respond to changing technology needs in direct producer-to-consumer marketing; or

children.label: i
children.text: obtaining food safety certification; and

children.label: ii
children.text: making changes and upgrades to practices and equipment to improve food safety.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish criteria and guidelines for the submission, evaluation, and funding of proposed projects under paragraph (1) as the Secretary determines are appropriate.

children.heading: Producer or food business benefits
children.label: B Producer or food business benefits

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), an application submitted for a grant under paragraph (1) shall include a description of the direct or indirect producer or food business benefits intended by the applicant to result from the proposed project within a reasonable period of time after the receipt of the grant.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply to a planning or feasibility project.

children.heading: Amount
children.label: 4 Amount
children.text: Unless otherwise determined by the Secretary, the amount of a grant under this subsection shall be not more than $500,000.

children.heading: Administration
children.label: A Administration
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out this subsection through the Administrator of the Rural Business-Cooperative Service, in coordination with the Administrator of the Agricultural Marketing Service.

children.label: i
children.text: an independent producer (as determined by the Secretary) of a value-added agricultural product; or

children.label: ii
children.text: an agricultural producer group, farmer or rancher cooperative, or majority-controlled producer-based business venture (as determined by the Secretary).

children.label: I
children.text: beginning farmers or ranchers;

children.label: II
children.text: socially disadvantaged farmers or ranchers;

children.label: III
children.text: operators of small or medium sized farms or ranches that are structured as family farms; or

children.label: IV
children.text: veteran farmers or ranchers; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of an application submitted by an eligible entity described in subparagraph (B)(ii), that provide the greatest contribution to creating or increasing marketing opportunities for producers described in subclauses (I) through (IV) of clause (i).

children.heading: Limitation on use of funds
children.label: D Limitation on use of funds

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), an eligible entity described in subparagraph (B) may not use a grant for the purchase or construction of a building, general purpose equipment, or structure.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: An eligible entity described in subparagraph (B) may use not more than $6,500 of the amount of a grant for an eligible activity described in paragraph (2)(J) to purchase or upgrade equipment to improve food safety.

children.heading: Matching funds
children.label: E Matching funds
children.text: An eligible entity described in subparagraph (B) receiving a grant shall contribute an amount of non-Federal funds that is at least equal to the amount of Federal funds received.

children.heading: Farmers’ markets and local food promotion program
children.label: 6 Farmers’ markets and local food promotion program

children.heading: Administration
children.label: A Administration
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out this subsection through the Administrator of the Agricultural Marketing Service, in coordination with the Administrator of the Rural Business-Cooperative Service.

children.label: i
children.text: an agricultural cooperative or other agricultural business entity or a producer network or association, including a community-supported agriculture network or association;

children.label: ii
children.text: a local or Tribal government;

children.label: iii
children.text: a nonprofit corporation;

children.label: iv
children.text: a public benefit corporation;

children.label: v
children.text: an economic development corporation;

children.label: vi
children.text: a regional farmers’ market authority;

children.label: vii
children.text: a food council; or

children.label: viii
children.text: such other entity as the Secretary may designate.

children.label: i
children.text: benefit underserved communities, including communities that are located in areas of concentrated poverty with limited access to fresh locally or regionally grown food; or

children.label: ii
children.text: are used to carry out eligible activities under a partnership agreement under subsection (e) and have not received benefits from the Program in the recent past.

children.heading: Limitation on use of funds
children.label: D Limitation on use of funds

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), an eligible entity described in subparagraph (B) may not use a grant for the purchase or construction of a building, general purpose equipment, or structure.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: An eligible entity described in subparagraph (B) may use not more than $6,500 of the amount of a grant for an eligible activity described in paragraph (2)(J) to purchase or upgrade equipment to improve food safety.

children.heading: Matching funds
children.label: E Matching funds
children.text: An eligible entity described in subparagraph (B) receiving a grant shall provide matching funds in the form of cash or an in-kind contribution in an amount that is equal to 25 percent of the total amount of the Federal portion of the grant.

children.label: i
children.text: a producer;

children.label: ii
children.text: a producer network or association;

children.label: iii
children.text: a farmer or rancher cooperative;

children.label: iv
children.text: a majority-controlled producer-based business venture;

children.label: v
children.text: a food council;

children.label: vi
children.text: a local or Tribal government;

children.label: vii
children.text: a nonprofit corporation;

children.label: viii
children.text: an economic development corporation;

children.label: ix
children.text: a public benefit corporation;

children.label: x
children.text: a community-supported agriculture network or association; and

children.label: xi
children.text: a regional farmers’ market authority.

children.label: i
children.text: a State agency or regional authority;

children.label: ii
children.text: a philanthropic organization;

children.label: iii
children.text: a private corporation;

children.label: iv
children.text: an institution of higher education;

children.label: v
children.text: a commercial, Federal, or Farm Credit System lending institution; and

children.label: vi
children.text: another entity, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Administrator of the Agricultural Marketing Service, in accordance with the purposes of the Program described in subsection (b), shall, using funds made available under subsection (i), provide grants for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2023 to support partnerships to plan and develop a local or regional food system.

children.heading: Geographical diversity
children.label: B Geographical diversity
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, the Secretary shall ensure geographical diversity in selecting partnerships to receive grants under subparagraph (A).

children.label: A
children.text: determine the scope of the regional food system to be developed, including goals, outreach objectives, and eligible activities to be carried out;

children.label: B
children.text: determine the local, regional, State, multi-State, or other geographic area covered;

children.label: C
children.text: create and conduct a feasibility study, implementation plan, and assessment of eligible activities under the partnership agreement;

children.label: D
children.text: conduct outreach and education to other eligible entities and eligible partners for potential participation in the partnership agreement and eligible activities;

children.label: E
children.text: describe measures to be taken through the partnership agreement to obtain funding for the eligible activities to be carried out under the partnership agreement;

children.label: F
children.text: at the request of a producer or eligible entity desiring to participate in eligible activities under the partnership agreement, act on behalf of the producer or eligible entity in applying for a grant under subsection (d);

children.label: G
children.text: monitor, evaluate, and periodically report to the Secretary on progress made toward achieving the objectives of eligible activities under the partnership agreement; or

children.label: i
children.text: the results and effects of the partnership agreement; and

children.label: ii
children.text: funds provided under paragraph (4).

children.heading: Contribution
children.label: 4 Contribution
children.text: A partnership receiving a grant under paragraph (2) shall provide funding in an amount equal to not less than 25 percent of the total amount of the Federal portion of the grant.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: To be eligible to receive a grant under paragraph (2), a partnership shall submit to the Secretary an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary considers necessary to evaluate and select applications.

children.label: i
children.text: shall conduct a competitive process to select applications submitted under subparagraph (A);

children.label: ii
children.text: may assess and rank applications with similar purposes as a group; and

children.label: iii
children.text: shall make public the criteria to be used in evaluating applications prior to accepting applications.

children.label: I
children.text: leverage significant non-Federal financial and technical resources; and

children.label: II
children.text: coordinate with other local, State, Tribal, or national efforts;

children.label: ii
children.text: cover an area that includes distressed low-income rural or urban communities, including areas with persistent poverty; or

children.label: iii
children.text: have multiple entities and partners in a partnership.

children.heading: Producer or food business benefits
children.label: D Producer or food business benefits

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), an application submitted under subparagraph (A) shall include a description of the direct or indirect producer or food business benefits intended by the eligible entity to result from the proposed project within a reasonable period of time after the receipt of a grant.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply to a planning or feasibility project.

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: 6 Technical assistance
children.text: On request of an eligible entity, an eligible partner, or a partnership, the Secretary may provide technical assistance in carrying out a partnership agreement.

children.heading: Simplification of application and reporting processes
children.label: f Simplification of application and reporting processes
A
children.text: request less than $50,000 under subsection (d); or

children.label: B
children.text: apply for grants under subsection (d) under a single application through partnership agreements under subsection (e).

children.label: A
children.text: streamline and simplify the reporting process for eligible entities; and

children.label: B
children.text: obtain from eligible entities and maintain such information as the Secretary determines is necessary to administer and evaluate the Program.

children.heading: Interdepartmental coordination
children.label: g Interdepartmental coordination
children.text: In carrying out the Program, to the maximum extent practicable, the Secretary shall ensure coordination among Federal agencies.

children.label: A
children.text: measures the economic impact of the Program on new and existing market outcomes;

children.label: i
children.text: the regional food economy through public and private partnerships;

children.label: ii
children.text: the production of value-added agricultural products;

children.label: iii
children.text: producer-to-consumer marketing, including direct producer-to-consumer marketing;

children.label: iv
children.text: local and regional food systems, including regional food chain coordination and business development;

children.label: v
children.text: new business opportunities and marketing strategies to reduce on-farm food waste;

children.label: vi
children.text: the use of new technologies in producer-to-consumer marketing, including direct producer-to-consumer marketing; and

children.label: vii
children.text: the workforce and capacity of regional food systems; and

children.label: i
children.text: each partnership agreement; and

children.label: ii
children.text: each grant provided under subsection (d).

children.heading: Report
children.label: 2 Report
children.text: Not later than 4 years after , the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate a report describing the evaluation conducted under paragraph (1), including a thorough analysis of the outcomes of the evaluation.December 20, 2018

children.heading: Mandatory funding
children.label: 1 Mandatory funding
children.text: Of the funds of the Commodity Credit Corporation, the Secretary shall use to carry out this section $50,000,000 for fiscal year 2019 and each fiscal year thereafter, to remain available until expended.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 2 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $20,000,000 for fiscal year 2019 and each fiscal year thereafter, to remain available until expended.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), of the funds made available to carry out this section for a fiscal year, 35 percent shall be used for grants under subsection (d)(5).

children.heading: Majority-controlled producer-based business ventures
children.label: I Majority-controlled producer-based business ventures
children.text: The total amount of grants under subsection (d)(5) provided to majority-controlled producer-based business ventures for a fiscal year shall not exceed 10 percent of the amount allocated under clause (i).

children.heading: Beginning, veteran, and socially disadvantaged farmers and ranchers
children.label: II Beginning, veteran, and socially disadvantaged farmers and ranchers
children.text: Of the funds made available for grants under subsection (d)(5), 10 percent shall be reserved for grants provided to beginning, veteran, and socially disadvantaged farmers or ranchers.

children.heading: Mid-tier value chains
children.label: III Mid-tier value chains
children.text: Of the funds made available for grants under subsection (d)(5), 10 percent shall be reserved for grants to develop mid-tier value chains.

children.heading: Food safety assistance
children.label: IV Food safety assistance
children.text: Of the funds made available for grants under subsection (d)(5), not more than 25 percent shall be reserved for grants for eligible activities described in subsection (d)(2)(J).

children.heading: Farmers’ market and local food promotion grants
children.label: B Farmers’ market and local food promotion grants
children.text: Of the funds made available to carry out this section for a fiscal year, 47 percent shall be used for grants under subsection (d)(6).

children.heading: Regional partnerships
children.label: C Regional partnerships
children.text: Of the funds made available to carry out this section for a fiscal year, 10 percent shall be used to provide grants to support partnerships under subsection (e).

children.label: i
children.text: shall be available to the agency carrying out the Program with the unobligated funds to carry out any function of the Program, as determined by the Secretary; and

children.label: ii
children.text: may carry over to the next fiscal year.

children.heading: Administrative expenses
children.label: E Administrative expenses
children.text: Not greater than 8 percent of amounts made available to provide grants under subsections (d) and (e) for a fiscal year may be used for administrative expenses.
